---
title: "White Beggarticks (Bidens alba)"
slug: "bidens-alba"
group: "Plantae"
image: "https://inaturalist-open-data.s3.amazonaws.com/photos/622273678/medium.jpg"        # keep for thumbnails
image-alt: "White Beggarticks"  # keep for a11y
tax_kingdom: "Plantae"
tax_class: "Magnoliopsida"
tax_order: "Asterales"
tax_family: "Asteraceae"
categories: ["Plantae", "Magnoliopsida", "Asterales", "Asteraceae"]   # these drive the chips + filtering
freeze: true
---

<figure class="species-figure">

<img src="https://inaturalist-open-data.s3.amazonaws.com/photos/622273678/medium.jpg" alt="White Beggarticks" class="species-photo"/>

<figcaption class="photo-credit">

Photo: <a href="https://www.inaturalist.org/people/jcarcache">
jcarcache</a> (CC BY-NC) via <a href="https://www.inaturalist.org/observations/341792498">iNaturalist</a>

</figcaption>

</figure>

## Taxonomy

-   **Class:** Magnoliopsida
-   **Order:** Asterales
-   **Family:** Asteraceae

## Occurrence at DeLuca Preserve

-   [DeLuca Bio Blitz Species Observations](https://www.inaturalist.org/observations?project_id=191727&subview=map&taxon_id=1095846)

## Conservation & rarity context

-   IUCN: Not Listed
-   **Rarity note:** Common species in Florida found in disturbed areas such as roadsides, fields, and gardens.

## Natural history notes

A fast-growing herb characterized by its opposite leaves and white daisy-like flowers with yellow centers. In Florida, it is commonly found in disturbed areas such as roadsides, fields, and gardens. It grows throughout much of the year in warm conditions and produces barbed seeds that readily stick to clothing or fur, aiding in dispersal.

## References / links

-   [iNaturalist species page](https://www.inaturalist.org/taxa/1095846-Bidens-alba)
-   [Local profile](https://florida.plantatlas.usf.edu/plant/species/3604)
